description_long: This is a large, amphora-style vase with a wide, rounded body and a narrow neck that flares outward at the rim. The vase features two prominent, curved handles that extend from the upper neck to the shoulder, providing both decorative and functional elements. Its surface is a matte, earthy brown, resembling unglazed terracotta, with subtle textural variations that suggest a hand-crafted, slightly rough finish. The body is unadorned by painted or carved motifs, emphasizing its classic, utilitarian form. The opening at the top is wide enough for floral arrangements or as a decorative container. This type of vase is reminiscent of ancient Mediterranean pottery, often seen in public gardens, plazas, or as street-side planters.
description: A large, brown terracotta vase with two curved handles and a wide, rounded body, featuring a matte, slightly rough surface. Its classic amphora shape and unadorned finish evoke traditional Mediterranean pottery.
description_view_0: Front view showing both curved handles and the wide, rounded body with a clear view of the neck and opening.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one handle in profile and the full curvature of the vase's body, with the neck and rim visible.
description_view_2: Opposite front view, similar to view 0, showing both handles and the open mouth, but with slightly different lighting.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the vase with one handle in profile and the broadest part of the body in shadow.
